description_long: This object is a detailed representation of a crustacean, specifically resembling a Norway lobster or scampi, with a segmented body featuring a robust cephalothorax and a long, tapering abdomen. The exoskeleton is a muted orange-pink color, covered in fine textures and small spines, giving it a rough, natural appearance. Prominent claws (chelae) are visible at the front, one noticeably larger than the other, and are accompanied by several pairs of articulated walking legs. Two long, slender antennae extend from the head, and the eyes are set on short stalks, providing a wide field of vision. The underside reveals the segmentation of the abdomen and the arrangement of the legs, while the dorsal view highlights the armored carapace and the symmetry of the appendages. The overall form is elongated and slightly curved, with a hard, chitinous shell typical of marine crustaceans.
description: A detailed, orange-pink crustacean with a segmented body, prominent claws, long antennae, and a hard, textured exoskeleton.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the body, the large and small claws, and the curvature of the abdomen.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the symmetry of the claws, the segmentation of the body, and the spread of the walking legs.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the other profile, the extended antennae, and the detailed texture of the exoskeleton.
description_view_3: Frontal view emphasizing the face, both claws, the long antennae, and the eyes on short stalks.
